framework.name: Agent Identity as Cryptographic Primitive
framework.description: Agent identity is established through cryptographic key pairs derived from seed passwords, enabling verifiable signatures and trust without centralized authentication. This suggests a move toward self-sovereign agent identity where agents prove who they are mathematically rather than through third-party verification.
framework.raw_quote: each agent is given a unique name and Seed password which is used to generate the agent's cryptographic key pair
